description:
To properly set the permissions of /etc/shells, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0644 /etc/shells
rationale:
The <tt>/etc/shells</tt> file contains the list of full pathnames to shells on the system.
Since this file is used by many system programs this file should be protected.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /etc/shells, run the command: 
$ ls -l /etc/shells
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: 0644
fixtext:
Configure the "/etc/shells" file to "0644" by running the following command:
$ sudo chmod 0644 /etc/shells
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/shells file must have a mode of 0640 to prevent unauthorized access.
title: Verify Permissions on /etc/shells File
